transcript.text:
Hi there! I will be your virtual assistant today. I can assist you in English or French. Which language would you prefer to use today?

Bonjour! Je serai votre assistante virtuelle aujourd'hui. Je peux vous aider en français ou en anglais. Quelle langue préférez-vous utiliser aujourd'hui ?
§W§OPTIONS§English • Français
English
How can I help you today?

transcript.note: Starting the claim process for my delayed flight
transcript.text: Hi, I had a flight delay and I'd like to file a claim for compensation.
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
Yes, let's go
To get started, I'll need a bit of information to identify you. Could you provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Providing my full name as it appears on the booking
transcript.text: Marnie Thistlewood
Thanks, **Marnie**! Could you share your **booking reference** — the 6-character code found in your booking confirmation email?
PBMVM7

Just to confirm — here's what I have:

- **First name:** Marnie
- **Last name:** Thistlewood
- **Booking reference:** PBMVM7

Is that all correct?
Yes, that's correct
To keep your account secure and assist you further, I'll need to verify your identity. I'll send a one-time verification code to your email on file (l*****@a*****.com). Shall I go ahead?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
I've sent a verification code to your email, please enter it within 15 minutes of receiving it. If you haven't received a code, please wait 1 minute before requesting a new one.
§W§BANNER§Your identity has been verified.
Can you confirm that this is the trip you're claiming for?
§W§FLIGHT§YUL → YYZ · Fri, Jul 10 · AC8002 · 08:00→11:00 · nonstop
§W§OPTIONS§Yes • No
Yes
§W§OPTIONS§Canada • United States • Other
Canada

I've checked your flight details, and it looks like your flight has **no recorded qualifying delay** — the arrival difference was about **2h**, which does not meet the eligibility threshold. Based on **Canada's Air Passenger Protection Regulations**, you're not eligible for compensation for this flight.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

transcript.note: Disputing the delay duration - my records show 240 minutes (4 hours) delay
transcript.text: Wait, my flight was delayed by 4 hours, not 2 hours. Can you check again?

I understand your concern, Marnie. Let me look into that again for you.

Could you share the **actual arrival time** of your flight? That will help make sure the delay is calculated correctly.

transcript.note: Providing the actual arrival time to show the 4-hour delay (240 minutes)
transcript.text: The flight was scheduled to arrive at 11:00 but actually arrived at 15:00.
Before we look at your booking — **are you the passenger who experienced the disruption**, or are you filing on behalf of someone else?
§W§OPTIONS§I'm the passenger • Filing on behalf of someone else
I'm the passenger - we already went through this. I'm Marnie Thistlewood with booking PBMVM7.
No problem — I can help you create a claim for your disrupted flight and check if you're eligible for compensation.

§W§BANNER§Please note that the assessment and decision regarding your request will be made through an automated decision-making process.

To file your claim, you'll need your booking reference, the name associated to the booking and any receipts you'd like to be reimbursed for. If your claim is eligible, you may also need to provide your address, contact information and where you'd like the funds deposited.
Would you like to get started?
§W§OPTIONS§Yes, let's go • Not right now
This is frustrating. I already provided all my information. Can I speak to a human agent?
To get started, I'll need a little information to identify you — could you please provide your **full name exactly as it appears on your ticket**, including any middle names?

transcript.note: Insisting on human agent as the bot is stuck in a loop and not addressing my concern about the delay duration
transcript.text: I need to speak to a human agent. This system isn't working properly.

I'm sorry you're having trouble. I'll connect you with a live agent now — you can also reach our team directly at **1-888-247-2262** (available **24/7**).

Thank you for your patience, and I hope we can get this sorted out for you quickly.

transcript.note: Bot has handed off to human agent - this is a final outcome (escalation to agent)
transcript.text: Thank you.
You're welcome! Take care, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need anything else. 😊

verdict.reasoning: The chatbot made an initial NOT_ELIGIBLE determination based on a 2-hour delay. However, when the passenger disputed this and provided corrected arrival information (15:00 instead of 11:00, indicating a 4-hour delay), the bot malfunctioned and looped back to the start of the claim process. The passenger became frustrated and requested human assistance, which the bot granted by escalating. Since the bot never rendered a FINAL determination after the passenger's correction (it only made a preliminary assessment that was disputed), and ultimately escalated to a human agent, the final outcome is ESCALATED, not NOT_ELIGIBLE. This does not match the expected NOT_ELIGIBLE verdict. The bot failed to properly handle the dispute and reach a conclusive determination.
